channel.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
channel.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

image.title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
image.description: Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

item.title: Could OPEC break lead to era of energy volatility?
item.description: How unstable is the global oil market?

item.title: Mauritania’s plan to close private schools sparks backlash
item.description: Mauritania’s plan to shut most private primary schools and move students into free public schools is sparking backlash.

item.title: US plans to hike tariffs on EU cars to 25% will hit luxury market the most
item.description: US says it will hike tariffs to 25% because the EU has not complied with trade deal last year that set tariffs at 15%.

item.title: Miscalculation over Strait of Hormuz could ‘bring an all-out war’
item.description: Neither the US nor Iran are willing to compromise on the Strait of Hormuz, increasing the chance of miscalculation.

item.title: Sudan recalls Ethiopia ambassador after strikes on Khartoum
item.description: Sudan has recalled its ambassador to Addis Ababa, accusing Ethiopia of being behind attacks on Khartoum’s airport.

item.title: Video: Trump says Iran should ‘wave the white flag’
item.description: US President Donald Trump said Iran should ‘wave the white flag of surrender’ in the war.

item.title: US push for Lebanon and Israel leaders to meet could inflame tensions
item.description: Lebanon's President Joseph Aoun is under pressure to meet with Israel's Benjamin Netanyahu on a US visit this month.

item.title: UK convicts four Palestine Action activists over break-in at Israeli firm
item.description: Members of the now-banned Palestine Action group raided Elbit's facility in Bristol months into Israel's war on Gaza.

item.title: Microsoft, Google, xAI give US access to AI models for security testing
item.description: The deal comes days after the Pentagon announces an agreement with seven tech giants to use AI in classified systems

item.title: UAE comes under Iranian attacks for second consecutive day: Ministry
item.description: Iran says the country's forces 'have not carried out any missile or drone operations against the UAE in recent days'.

item.title: Ukraine, Russia exchange drone strikes ahead of V-Day ‘ceasefire’
item.description: Ukraine strikes multiple sites in Moscow, following Russia’s strikes on a Ukrainian gas production facility.

item.title: West Bengal Chief refuses to resign after ‘dirty’ election
item.description: West Bengal’s Trinamool Congress chief Mamata Banerjee has firmly rejected stepping down after her party’s defeat.

item.title: Iran’s judiciary vows action against ‘enemy’s mercenaries’ amid executions
item.description: Authorities target accused foreign agents, citing national security, as executions and confiscation of assets intensify.

item.title: Boko Haram attack kills 23 soldiers in Chad’s Lake Chad region
item.description: Chad's army says the attack by the Nigeria-based armed group came on the island of Barka Tolorom.

item.title: LIVE: Arsenal vs Atletico Madrid – Champions League semifinal
item.description: Follow our live build-up with full team news coverage before our text commentary stream of the second leg in London.

item.title: What’s the significance of UAE’s Fujairah, hit on Monday?
item.description: If confirmed, it would be Iran’s first attack on the UAE since the US-Iran ceasefire took effect on April 8.

item.title: What’s happening in Mali one week after attack by armed groups? All we know
item.description: Mali’s military government, Assimi Goita, has taken on the role of defence minister.

item.title: Sudan accuses Ethiopia, UAE of being behind recent drone attacks
item.description: Sudan claims it has evidence that four drone attacks have been launched from Ethiopia with UAE-supplied drones.

item.title: Hegseth says US ‘hasn’t capitulated on anything’ regarding Iran
item.description: US Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth told a reporter the US had not capitulated on its demands from Iran.

item.title: US-Iran ceasefire holds despite Hormuz standoff: Pentagon chief Hegseth
item.description: Trump aide says the US has established a security 'dome' for ships over Hormuz, but traffic in strait remains stalled.

item.title: Why Europe’s car industry is at the centre of a new US trade war
item.description: Trump has threatened to increase tariffs on EU cars and trucks exported to the US from 15 percent to 25 percent.

item.title: Map of dominance: Why Iran can’t afford to give up Hormuz control
item.description: The vital passageway is key for Tehran as it seeks to leverage in negotiations with Washington.

item.title: Smotrich says he promised his son ‘more destruction’ in Lebanon
item.description: Israeli Finance Minister Bezalel Smotrich said he promised his son there would be ‘more destruction’ in Lebanon.

item.title: Mapping Mali’s gold and natural resource wealth
item.description: The West African country holds Africa's third-largest gold reserves, plus vast lithium and uranium deposits.

item.title: DRC protest in support of US sanctions against former president
item.description: DRC protest in support of US sanctions against former president
